title: 🎙️ Community Tech & Governance
description: Today we look at how Cariboo communities govern themselves and use technology for public good — from school district digital initiatives and rural broadband projects to council decisions and civic innovation. We're interested in stories where technology and local democracy intersect, and how small municipalities are solving big problems with limited resources. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today we look at how Cariboo communities govern themselves and use technology for public good — from school district digital initiatives and rural broadband projects to council decisions and civic innovation. We're interested in stories where technology and local democracy intersect, and how small municipalities are solving big problems with limited resources.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Community Tech & Governance' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: municipal broadband and fiber projects in Cariboo communities, SD27 education technology and digital learning, civic tech platforms and open data initiatives, community-owned networks and ISPs, smart rural infrastructure (water, waste, transit), digital government services for rural areas, council decisions on technology spending, public Wi-Fi and connectivity programs, election technology and civic engagement platforms, rural innovation hubs, and how Williams Lake/Quesnel/100 Mile/CRD use technology for public services. Prefer articles that bridge governance AND technology in rural civic contexts. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [Science-Based Medicine] Zoonotic Spillover Is A Problem
items.content_html:
Recently I gave the latest update on the origins of SARS-CoV-2 – bottom line, it was very likely a zoonotic spillover event and not a lab leak. Following that I interviewed a researcher, Dr. Andersen, who is an expert on the origins of epidemics/pandemics and has researched this very question. He reinforced the spillover hypothesis, indicated he had considered the lab leak […]

items.title: [The Guardian Global Development] How weaving, glamping and kayak tours are helping to tackle deforestation in Argentina’s Gran Chaco
items.content_html:
Small farmers and community-led conservation groups are trying to protect one of the biggest semi-arid forests in the world – under threat from expanding agriculture, wildfires and the ‘logging mafia’

Jorge Luna stands in a piece of Argentina’s Gran Chaco forest that he calls his own. Birds sing as he surveys skyscraping molle trees, known as pepper trees, palo santo and algarrobo, or carob trees. “It’s good wood,” says Luna, 55. “I was about to cut them down.”

Selling timber promises quick and easy money in the sprawling ecosystem that covers parts of Argentina, Bolivia, Paraguay and Brazil. But it comes at a steep price, contributing to rampant deforestation and irreversible damage to the forest.

Continue reading...
